1→"""Tests for @machine_command that depend on the erk CLI tree.""" 2→ 3→import click 4→ 5→from erk.cli.cli import cli 6→ 7→ 8→def test_machine_commands_exist_in_json_tree() -> None: 9→ """At least one @machine_command exists under erk json.""" 10→ json_group = cli.commands.get("json") 11→ assert json_group is not None, "erk json group not found in CLI tree" 12→ 13→ def _collect_machine_commands(group: click.Command) -> list[click.Command]: 14→ result = [] 15→ if hasattr(group, "_machine_command_meta"): 16→ result.append(group) 17→ if isinstance(group, click.Group): 18→ for cmd in group.commands.values(): 19→ result.extend(_collect_machine_commands(cmd)) 20→ return result 21→ 22→ commands = _collect_machine_commands(json_group) 23→ assert len(commands) > 0, "No @machine_command commands found under erk json" 24→

1→"""Machine command decorator for pure-JSON CLI commands. 2→ 3→Provides @machine_command for commands that: 4→- Accept JSON input from stdin (not Click params) 5→- Return structured JSON output 6→- Use frozen dataclass request types for strict validation 7→- Support --schema flag for introspection 8→ 9→Unlike @json_command (which adds --json/--schema to human commands), 10→@machine_command creates dedicated machine-only commands with no 11→Click-based input parsing. The request type IS the input schema. 12→""" 13→ 14→import json 15→from dataclasses import dataclass 16→from typing import Any 17→ 18→import click 19→ 20→from erk_shared.agentclick.dataclass_json import ( 21→ emit_json_error, 22→ emit_json_success, 23→ parse_dataclass_from_json, 24→ read_json_stdin, 25→ serialize_to_json_dict, 26→) 27→ 28→ 29→@dataclass(frozen=True) 30→class MachineCommandMeta:
... [188 lines omitted] ...
38→class MachineCommandError: 39→ """Structured error result from a machine command.""" 41→ error_type: str 59→ to_json_dict()) or a MachineCommandError. The decorator handles 79→ json.JSONDecodeError: If stdin contains invalid JSON 80→ ValueError: If stdin JSON is not an object 99→ ValueError: On validation errors (unknown fields, missing required, type mismatch) 104→def emit_machine_error(error: MachineCommandError) -> None: 105→ """Emit a structured error to stdout.""" 106→ emit_json_error(error_type=error.error_type, message=error.message) 152→ except json.JSONDecodeError as exc: 153→ emit_machine_error( 154→ MachineCommandError( 155→ error_type="invalid_json_input", 160→ except ValueError as exc: 161→ emit_machine_error( 162→ MachineCommandError( 163→ error_type="invalid_json_input", 175→ except ValueError as exc: 176→ emit_machine_error( 177→ MachineCommandError( 178→ error_type="invalid_request", 188→ if isinstance(result, MachineCommandError): 189→ emit_machine_error(result) 194→ except click.ClickException as exc: 195→ error_type_attr = getattr(exc, "error_type", None) 196→ if error_type_attr is not None: 197→ emit_machine_error( 198→ MachineCommandError( 199→ error_type=str(error_type_attr),

session.tool_result: packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/agentclick/dataclass_json.py:249:def emit_json_success(data: dict[str, Any]) -> None: /home/runner/work/erk/erk/packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/agentclick/dataclass_json.py-250- """Emit a JSON success result to stdout. Adds success=True automatically.""" /home/runner/work/erk/erk/packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/agentclick/dataclass_json.py-251- data["success"] = True /home/runner/work/erk/erk/packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/agentclick/dataclass_json.py-252- click.echo(json.dumps(data, indent=2)) /home/runner/work/erk/erk/packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/agentclick/dataclass_json.py-253- /home/runner/work/erk/erk/packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/agentclick/dataclass_json.py-254- packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/agentclick/dataclass_json.py-255-def emit_json_error(*, error_type: str, message: str) -> None: /home/runner/work/erk/erk/packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/agentclick/dataclass_json.py-256- """Emit a structured error to stdout.""" packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/agentclick/dataclass_json.py-257- data = {"success": False, "error_type": error_type, "message": message} /home/runner/work/erk/erk/packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/agentclick/dataclass_json.py-258- click.echo(json.dumps(data, indent=2)) /home/runner/work/erk/erk/packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/agentclick/dataclass_json.py-259-
session.assistant: I see — `emit_json_success` flattens the data dict and adds `success=True` directly to it, not wrapped in a `data` key. So the output is `{"success": true, "pr_number": 42, ...}` not `{"success": true, "data": {...}}`. Let me fix the test:
